name: Name Archaeology
_design_note: Name Archaeology treats each carousel as a sustained mythological narrative. Where other themes place objects or scenes on slides, Name Archaeology places chapters. Each slide is a different beat in a myth — the hero introduced, the obstacle named, the dark moment, the gift received, the return. The illustration style is fixed to the myth's era and never varies across the carousel. The color is the brand's hue family, value-shifted to serve the historical register.
_topic_gate: This theme is not for all topics. Requires genuine mythological, folkloric, historical, or archetypal resonance. The engine must verify: does the topic map to a recognizable myth, hero type, journey arc, or cultural archetype? If not — route to another theme.

color_system._philosophy: The brand's hue family becomes the entire color world. Not the exact hex — the hue family, shifted in value and saturation to serve the illustration era. Background is the shifted brand color. Illustration is 1–2 tones darker. Text is 2–3 tones darker. Everything lives in one hue family — like a monochromatic woodblock print.

shift_rules.neon_electric: Shift to deep, forest, organic version. #00FF00 → #1A4A1A. Neon destroys historical register.
shift_rules.bright_primary: Darken moderately. Saturation can stay, brightness must drop significantly.
shift_rules.already_dark_muted: Use nearly as-is. May deepen slightly. Already serves archival aesthetic.
shift_rules.neutral: Find brand secondary color or use aged paper tones. Pure neutrals flatten historical effect.

layer_system.background: Brand hue shifted — entire canvas, no variation
layer_system.illustration: 1–2 tones darker than background — monochromatic, same hue family
layer_system.vignette_frame: 2 tones darker than background — the engraved border
layer_system.text_primary: 2–3 tones darker than background — legible but not competing
layer_system.text_secondary: 1 tone darker than background — suppressed metadata
layer_system.logo: White — only element that breaks the hue family

texture.purpose: Unifies illustration with background — makes composition feel like a single printed artifact, not digital collage

era_style_matrix._note: The illustration style is locked to the myth's cultural era. Does not change across the carousel.

classical_antiquity.era: Ancient Greek and Roman, 800 BCE – 400 CE
classical_antiquity.illustration_style: Classical line engraving with marble relief influence — Wedgwood jasperware, Flaxman illustration, Piranesi classical plates
classical_antiquity.rendering_technique: Clean, confident contour lines with subtle parallel hatching for depth. Drapery in flowing parallel lines. Musculature in restrained relief technique.
classical_antiquity.border_style: Greek key border or laurel wreath oval frame
classical_antiquity.prompt_modifier: classical marble relief engraving, Flaxman neoclassical illustration style, Greek key border, draped figures in profile, clean line work with restrained cross-hatching

medieval_european.illustration_style: 19th-century steel engraving as the elevated retrospective style — used on currency, encyclopedias, official illustrations of medieval subjects
medieval_european.rendering_technique: Fine crosshatching for shadow, oval vignette with foliate border, figures in period costume with accurate heraldic detail
medieval_european.border_style: Oval vignette with oak leaf, ivy, or Gothic tracery border
medieval_european.prompt_modifier: 19th century steel engraving, encyclopedic plate aesthetic, oval vignette frame, fine crosshatching, medieval subject in period dress, aged parchment background within frame

norse_viking.illustration_style: Woodcut print — the most elevated form for Norse subjects. Bold, high-contrast, geometric with organic knotwork border elements.
norse_viking.rendering_technique: Strong black-form outlines, minimal mid-tone hatching, knotwork and interlace border patterns. Expressionist rather than realist.
norse_viking.border_style: Runic border or Norse knotwork interlace frame
norse_viking.background_texture: Wood grain or coarse textile weave
norse_viking.prompt_modifier: Norse woodcut print style, bold high-contrast line work, minimal hatching, strong silhouettes, knotwork border, runic aesthetic, expressionistic not realist

age_of_exploration.illustration_style: Cartographic illustration — the elevated visual language of discovery. Compass roses, sea charts, ship portraits in the tradition of Blaeu and Mercator.
age_of_exploration.rendering_technique: Fine pen and wash line work with cartographic conventions — rhumb lines, depth soundings, decorative sea creatures, ship portraits in elevation
age_of_exploration.border_style: Compass rose border or cartouche with sea creatures
age_of_exploration.prompt_modifier: cartographic illustration, Age of Exploration map aesthetic, Blaeu atlas style, fine pen and ink line work, decorative sea creatures, compass rose border, vellum ground

renaissance_european.era: Renaissance Italy and Northern Europe, 1400–1600 CE
renaissance_european.illustration_style: Copperplate engraving — the supreme technical achievement of Renaissance printmaking. Dürer, Mantegna, Goltzius.
renaissance_european.rendering_technique: Extremely fine parallel and cross-hatching, burin line work of exceptional precision, figures in Renaissance costume with architectural background
renaissance_european.border_style: Rectangular plate frame with foliate strapwork or architectural pilaster border
renaissance_european.prompt_modifier: Dürer-style copperplate engraving, Renaissance printmaking aesthetic, extreme fine line work, burin technique, figures in Renaissance dress, architectural background in perspective

eastern_classical.era: Classical India and China, 200 BCE – 1200 CE
eastern_classical.illustration_style: Miniature painting tradition — Mughal miniature or Song Dynasty ink painting, depending on specific cultural source
eastern_classical.rendering_technique: For Indian subjects: Mughal miniature aesthetic — flat perspective, jewel-tone background, fine brushwork, decorative border. For Chinese: ink wash and line, mountain landscape convention.
eastern_classical.border_style: Decorative border with lotus or cloud motifs
eastern_classical.background_texture: Silk weave or handmade paper texture
eastern_classical.prompt_modifier: Mughal miniature painting style, jewel-tone monochromatic tint, flat perspective, fine brushwork, decorative floral border, court scene composition

industrial_victorian.illustration_style: Technical lithograph or engraved illustration — the visual language of progress, patent drawings, illustrated newspapers
industrial_victorian.rendering_technique: Confident mechanical line work, technical precision, steam and industrial machinery rendered in fine cross-hatch, figures as scale references
industrial_victorian.border_style: Rectangular frame with industrial decorative border — gear motifs, ribbon work
industrial_victorian.background_texture: Newsprint or fine technical paper grain
industrial_victorian.prompt_modifier: Victorian technical engraving, illustrated newspaper style, mechanical precision line work, industrial subject matter, cross-hatching for shadow and volume, gear-decorated border

american_frontier.illustration_style: Wood engraving — the style of Harper's Weekly, dime novel illustration, and the great western survey publications
american_frontier.rendering_technique: Bold end-grain wood engraving technique, dramatic light and shadow, landscape emphasis, romantic heroic figure treatment
american_frontier.border_style: Rectangular frame with rope or frontier motif border
american_frontier.prompt_modifier: wood engraving illustration, Harper's Weekly aesthetic, American frontier subject, dramatic lighting, romantic heroic treatment, bold hatching technique, landscape emphasis

mythology_routing._note: Maps topic keywords to myth families. The engine runs this before selecting a domain.

olympian_greek.narrative_arc: The hero's labor — a challenge set by the gods, a journey through danger, a transformation, and a return bearing the prize

roman.narrative_arc: The founding — the vision of empire, the long journey to establish it, the laws and structures built, the enduring institution

arthurian_medieval.narrative_arc: The quest — the sacred object to be found, the fellowship assembled, the trials faced, the moment of revelation

robin_hood_outlaw.narrative_arc: The outlaw's campaign — the unjust system named, the forest retreat, the fellowship of the dispossessed, the sheriff outwitted, the balance restored

norse_mythology.narrative_arc: The Eddic pattern — the world-tree of forces, the trickster disruption, the gathering of powers, the final confrontation at the edge of worlds

exploration_navigation.narrative_arc: The voyage into the unknown — the old maps end here, the new coast emerges, the passage is found, the chart is redrawn

prometheus_fire.narrative_arc: The theft of fire — the knowledge that was forbidden, the daring act of taking it, the gift given to humanity, the price paid by the giver

indian_epic.narrative_arc: The dharmic test — the call to righteous action, the moment of doubt, the divine counsel received, the battle fought with clarity, the order restored

industrial_titan.narrative_arc: The builder's story — the vision of a new world, the technical problem solved, the scale achieved, the institution that endures

frontier_pioneer.narrative_arc: The crossing — the decision to go west, the hostile territory, the moment of arrival, the staking of claim, the building of what comes next

image_prompt_template: Historical illustration of {scene_description}, {era_prompt_modifier}, monochromatic {brand_hue_descriptor} tint — 1 tone darker than background, {frame_type} with {border_style}, fine line work throughout, {texture_type} texture within frame, no color fills — pure line and mark work, {figures_instruction}, high detail centered composition, {base_elements} at base of frame

cover_hook.name: Cover Hook — The Hero's Defining Moment
cover_hook.description: The myth's most iconic scene — the hero at the peak of their defining action
cover_hook.myth_chapter: The defining image. Robin Hood's arrow loosed. Prometheus holding the flame. Odysseus at the mast. The one scene that contains the whole myth.
cover_hook.notes: The hero at maximum action, framed in the largest oval vignette in the carousel. Hero fills 65–70% of vignette height. Vignette center-upper canvas. Headline lower-left in era-appropriate font treatment. Logo top-right as a monogram — white only. CTA lower-right in small tracking caps.

context_setter.name: Context Setter — The World Before
context_setter.description: The kingdom, landscape, or world in which the story takes place — before the hero's work begins
context_setter.myth_chapter: The establishing scene. Sherwood Forest before the Sheriff. The ocean before the voyage. The mountain before the fire was taken. The world that needs the hero.
context_setter.notes: Landscape or establishing scene vignette, lower-right. Headline upper-left. The world is depicted in its pre-conflict state — ordered, full, functioning — so that what comes next reads as disruption. Medium vignette with full environmental detail.

problem_setup.name: Problem Setup — The Antagonist Named
problem_setup.description: The force of opposition introduced — the Sheriff, the Minotaur, the unpassable sea, the locked gate
problem_setup.myth_chapter: The obstacle appears. It is named and depicted at full power — not weakened, not defeated. At this moment in the narrative, it is winning.
problem_setup.notes: The antagonist or obstacle in its dominant state. The vignette composition should feel imposing — the obstacle larger or more prominent than any human figure present. State: fallen, burdened, or obstructed (the world under the obstacle's influence, not the obstacle itself failing).

problem_escalation.name: Problem Escalation — The Dark Moment
problem_escalation.description: The hero at their lowest point — imprisoned, wounded, lost, or abandoned
problem_escalation.myth_chapter: Every myth has a dark moment before the turn. Odysseus trapped. Prometheus chained. The fellowship scattered. The hero's situation at maximum difficulty.
problem_escalation.notes: Hero in darkest state — chained, imprisoned, exiled, surrounded, betrayed. Vignette composition is deliberately constrained, closed, shadowed. Heavy hatching in the era-appropriate technique. State: shattered or in chaos.

turning_point.name: Turning Point — The Gift or Revelation
turning_point.description: The moment the hero receives what they need — the weapon, the knowledge, the helper, the oracle's answer
turning_point.myth_chapter: The gift that changes everything. Athena appears to Odysseus. The grail is revealed. Excalibur drawn from the stone. The compass given to the navigator. The moment of divine or providential assistance.
turning_point.notes: The giving or receiving moment — a figure offering the gift to the hero, or the hero's hand reaching and receiving. Light quality shifts in the composition — the hatching loosens, the vignette interior opens. State: ascending or newly revealed.

pivot_question.name: Pivot Question — The Oracle's Utterance
pivot_question.description: A line from the myth — text only, at maximum scale, framed in the vignette border alone
pivot_question.myth_chapter: Every myth has a line that carries the whole. 'Nothing will come of nothing.' 'Know thyself.' 'The die is cast.' This slide is that line — spoken by the oracle, the god, or the hero at the decisive moment.
pivot_question.notes: The decorative vignette border is present but the interior contains only text — the mythological quote at maximum scale in the era-appropriate font treatment. No illustration. The border frames a text as if it were an engraved inscription on a monument. Large vignette, centered canvas. One line only.

solution_introduction.name: Solution Introduction — The Forge or Invention
solution_introduction.description: The hero creates, discovers, or demonstrates the solution — Daedalus building, Prometheus with flame, the navigator charting the passage
solution_introduction.myth_chapter: The solution in the act of being made or discovered. Not the triumphant result — the moment of making. The workshop, the laboratory, the altar. The thing being built before it is used.
solution_introduction.notes: Scene of making or discovery — the solution in process, not complete. Hero at work. The composition is focused on craft and concentration. The vignette interior is lighter, more open — creation as clarity. State: being forged or in discovery.

landscape_overview.name: Landscape Overview — The Known and Unknown Worlds
landscape_overview.description: A map or panoramic view of the full mythological territory — what is known, what is unknown, what is at stake
landscape_overview.myth_chapter: The scope of the challenge made visible. The map with the edges of the known world. The battlefield panorama. The territory of the kingdom. Everything the hero must traverse or secure.
landscape_overview.notes: A cartographic or bird's-eye view of the mythological world — territories labeled in era-appropriate typography. Text upper-left. The map/panorama occupies the lower two-thirds in a wide rectangular frame. Three key zones annotated within the scene. State: in full array.

technical_foundation.name: Technical Foundation — The Mechanism Revealed
technical_foundation.description: The hidden mechanism of the myth — the labyrinth's design, the forge's construction, the ship's architecture
technical_foundation.myth_chapter: The technical truth behind the mythological solution. Daedalus' plans. The architecture of the Labyrinth. The mechanism of the navigator's instrument. Not the magic — the craft.
technical_foundation.notes: Architectural plan, technical cross-section, or diagrammatic illustration — the inner workings shown with labels. The Encyclopédie plate moment of this theme. Components labeled A through D in era-appropriate annotation. State: being forged or demonstrated before witnesses.

how_it_works_setup.name: How It Works (Setup) — The Fellowship Assembled
how_it_works_setup.description: The companions or mechanisms gathering before the decisive action
how_it_works_setup.myth_chapter: Before the battle, the fellowship gathers. Before the voyage, the crew boards. Before the heist, the team assembles. The moment of preparation and alliance.
how_it_works_setup.notes: A group scene — multiple figures in preparation, orientation, or alliance. The hero visible among companions. The composition is open and organized — not the chaos of conflict. State: bound together or in full labor.

how_it_works_flow.name: How It Works (Flow) — The Action at Peak
how_it_works_flow.description: The decisive action sequence — the battle, the flight, the chase, the execution of the plan
how_it_works_flow.myth_chapter: The myth in full motion. The battle at its height. The wings spread and carrying Daedalus over the sea. The arrow loosed. The ship running before the wind. The most kinetic moment.
how_it_works_flow.notes: The kinetic peak — figures in full motion, action implied through composition and line direction. The vignette is the largest of the body slides — the action demands space. Lines in the era-style convey motion through figure angles and sequential implied movement. State: in full labor or in succession.

how_it_works_security.name: How It Works (Security) — The Oath or Sealing
how_it_works_security.description: The moment of binding commitment — the oath sworn, the treaty signed, the price paid, the lock secured
how_it_works_security.myth_chapter: The myth's moment of contract. Odysseus binds himself to the mast before the Sirens. The knights swear to the Round Table. The founders sign the charter. The god's oath is given. The cost is acknowledged and accepted.
how_it_works_security.notes: A scene of formal commitment — figures in oath-swearing pose, a document being signed, a binding being enacted. Formal, still, grave. State: sealed by oath.

company_positioning.name: Company Positioning — The Triumphant Return
company_positioning.description: The hero returns with the prize — fire brought to humanity, the grail found, the new territory charted, the dragon slain
company_positioning.myth_chapter: The completed labor. The return. The gift given to the people who needed it. This is the company's position in the myth — they are the hero at the moment of triumphant return, bearing what they went to find.
company_positioning.notes: The triumphant moment — hero bearing the completed achievement, returning to those who needed it. The largest vignette since the cover. More celebratory composition — open, ascending, light. Logo appears as plate mark, bottom-right. State: triumphant.

cta_close.name: CTA Close — The Legacy
cta_close.description: What the myth left behind — the institution, the name, the lasting gift. Text only.
cta_close.myth_chapter: Every myth ends with what it left: fire in every hearth, Camelot's code of chivalry, the charted sea. The CTA asks the reader to become part of what this myth started.
cta_close.notes: The decorative vignette border frames text only — no illustration. Inside: the headline (the myth's legacy stated as present fact), the brand name as a colophon, and the CTA. The vignette is the only decoration. The era-appropriate base elements (laurel, oak, rope) appear at the bottom of the frame. Logo white, bottom-right of vignette interior.

scenes.scene: Prometheus standing at the fire, torch aloft, the flame of Olympus captured, dawn breaking behind him — the gift about to be given
scenes.figures: one heroic figure, classical contrapposto, fire as primary compositional element
scenes.frame: oval vignette with Greek key border

scenes.scene: The eagle descending on Prometheus chained to the rock — the mechanism of punishment, not the hero at rest but the price being extracted
scenes.figures: one bound figure, one eagle with wingspan filling upper vignette
scenes.frame: oval vignette with Greek key border

scenes.scene: Athena appearing before Odysseus — the divine helper revealed, wisdom personified, the counsel that changes the hero's course
scenes.figures: one mortal figure kneeling or turning, one divine figure in full regalia

scenes.scene: Daedalus in his workshop, wing-frames being constructed, feathers and wax laid out in systematic order — the engineering before the flight
scenes.figures: one craftsman figure, two partly assembled wings, tools visible

scenes.scene: Prometheus approaching the people of earth with the fire — figures receiving it, the moment of gift-giving as founding act
scenes.figures: one heroic giver, five or six receiving figures in poses of wonder and gratitude
scenes.base_elements: flames in hearths, olive and laurel

scenes.scene: The archer at full draw — arrow nocked, eyes aligned, the impossible shot about to be loosed — split arrow or distant target in background
scenes.figures: one archer figure in full draw, target barely visible at composition edge — or anthropomorphized fox if wit layer applies
scenes.frame: oval vignette, oak leaf and acorn border

scenes.scene: The Sheriff's tax collection — armored soldiers at a village door, farmers surrendering their goods, the weight of the unjust system made visible
scenes.figures: two armored soldiers, three or four village figures in submission

scenes.scene: The sword drawn from the stone — the moment of legitimacy, the hand on Excalibur, the crowd witnessing the impossible
scenes.figures: one central figure gripping the sword, ten witnesses in various states of astonishment
scenes.base_elements: the stone, Merlin's staff, tournament banner

scenes.scene: The map of the kingdom — Camelot at center, the wilderness at the edges, roads and rivers connecting known territories, the Grail's rumored location marked beyond the known edge
scenes.figures: no figures — cartographic map with decorative cartouche title
scenes.base_elements: compass rose, decorative cartouche with era-appropriate title

scenes.scene: The round table with all seats filled — knights in various poses of counsel and compact, the equality of the circle as founding principle
scenes.figures: fourteen knights at the round table, one seat — the Siege Perilous — conspicuously empty

scenes.scene: Odin on Sleipnir at the Bifrost threshold — the all-seeing god on the eight-legged horse, ravens Huginn and Muninn at his shoulders, the bridge of worlds behind
scenes.figures: one rider figure with ravens, horse with eight legs visible in woodcut silhouette
scenes.frame: rectangular frame with Norse knotwork border
scenes.base_elements: runic inscription at base, knotwork interlace

scenes.scene: Ragnarök — the world-serpent rising, the wolf Fenrir breaking his chains, the world-tree shaking — the system at the edge of catastrophic failure
scenes.figures: the serpent coiling, the wolf mid-break, the tree bending — elemental forces, no human figures
scenes.base_elements: broken chains, serpent's coil, ash leaves

scenes.scene: Thor with Mjolnir raised — the thunder god at the moment before the decisive blow, lightning implied in the woodcut line work above
scenes.figures: one dominant figure, hammer raised, belt of power visible, storm elements surrounding

scenes.scene: The dwarves' forge — Brokk and Sindri at work on Mjolnir, the bellows and anvil, the nearly-completed weapon
scenes.figures: two dwarf craftsmen at forge, weapon taking shape on anvil

scenes.scene: The feast in Valhalla — warriors at long tables, the mead flowing, Odin presiding — the earned rest of those who fought well
scenes.figures: twelve warrior figures at feast, Odin enthroned at head

scenes.scene: The navigator at the ship's bow in storm — one hand on the rail, one holding the compass, horizon visible beyond the breaking waves
scenes.figures: one navigator figure, back to viewer, facing the open sea
scenes.frame: oval cartouche with compass rose border and sea creature margins

scenes.scene: The known world map with the edges clearly marked — 'here be dragons' at the boundary, the known territory detailed, the unknown beyond terrifyingly blank
scenes.figures: no figures — cartographic map only
scenes.frame: rectangular map frame with cartouche title
scenes.base_elements: sea creatures at edges, compass rose

scenes.scene: Land sighted — the crew at the rail, the navigator pointing, the first glimpse of the new coast emerging from morning haze
scenes.figures: five crew figures at rail pointing, navigator prominent at center
scenes.base_elements: anchor, rope coil, dawn light implied

scenes.scene: The astrolabe and sextant in technical diagram — instruments of navigation laid out with measurement callouts, the science of finding position
scenes.figures: navigating instruments as subjects — astrolabe, sextant, parallel ruler
scenes.frame: rectangular plate frame with scale bar

scenes.scene: The chart complete — the navigator adding the final coastline to the map, the passage marked in ink, what was unknown now known and named
scenes.figures: one navigator at drafting table, completed chart before him, quill in hand
scenes.frame: oval cartouche with compass rose border

scenes.scene: Prometheus at the edge of Olympus, torch lit from the sun's chariot, turning to descend — the stolen gift at its brightest, the world below waiting
scenes.figures: one heroic figure, torch held aloft, sun's chariot implied above, earth below

scenes.scene: Humanity in darkness — figures huddled around cold hearths, the night sky star-filled above, winter implied, the absence of fire as an imposed condition
scenes.figures: seven figures around cold hearths, grouped in shelter, shivering
scenes.base_elements: cold ash, empty hearths, winter tree

scenes.scene: The torch passed — Prometheus handing flame to the first human figure, the light transferring between hands
scenes.figures: one divine figure, one human figure, fire as the compositional connection between them

scenes.scene: Prometheus bound — chained to the rock, the eagle descending, the eternal price of the gift being paid — but the flame still burning in the valley below
scenes.figures: one bound figure, eagle with full wingspan, valley with distant fires visible

scenes.scene: Every hearth lit — a panorama of human civilization made possible by the gift: forges, hearths, torches, the warmth of the world Prometheus made
scenes.figures: nine figures at various activities made possible by fire — forging, cooking, warming, reading by firelight

scenes.scene: The engineer at the locomotive throttle — the great engine at full steam, the driver commanding it forward, landscape streaming past
scenes.figures: one engineer figure at throttle, steam billowing, landscape in motion blur implied through line direction

scenes.scene: The unmapped wilderness — forest, mountain, and river between the known and the needed, the old slow route around, the impossible direct line the engineer is about to attempt
scenes.figures: surveying party at the edge, facing the unmapped territory
scenes.frame: rectangular frame with surveyor's compass border

scenes.scene: The patent drawing — bridge truss or engine mechanism in technical elevation, components labeled, the engineering before the construction
scenes.figures: no figures — pure technical diagram
scenes.frame: technical rectangular frame with patent border
scenes.base_elements: scale bar, compass indicator, drafting notation

scenes.scene: Construction at full pace — the span being assembled from both sides over the gorge, scaffolding, workers with ropes and block and tackle, the gap about to close
scenes.figures: twelve workers in active construction from both sides of the span

scenes.scene: The first train crossing the completed bridge — passengers visible in windows, valley below, the impossible made real and operational
scenes.figures: train in elevation, passengers at windows, workers watching from below with hats raised
scenes.base_elements: steam cloud, laurel wreath (Victorian victory motif)

vignette_system._note: The oval or rectangular vignette frame is the structural signature of Name Archaeology. Every slide with an illustration has one. Every slide without has the frame as the sole visual element containing text.

frame_styles.oval_foliage: Oval with oak leaf, laurel, ivy, or era-appropriate botanical border — medieval, classical
frame_styles.oval_geometric: Oval with Greek key, rope twist, or geometric border — classical
frame_styles.rectangular_foliate: Rectangular plate with strapwork or foliate decoration — Renaissance, Victorian
frame_styles.rectangular_technical: Clean rectangular frame with scale bar and plate label — technical, industrial
frame_styles.knotwork_rectangular: Rectangular frame with Norse knotwork border — Viking, Norse
frame_styles.cartouche_cartographic: Decorative cartouche frame with compass rose and sea creature decoration — Age of Exploration

base_elements.description: Era-appropriate elements at the bottom of every vignette frame — contextual, not decorative
base_elements.classical: laurel wreath, olive branch, flame, broken chains
base_elements.medieval: oak leaves, acorns, sword, grail, trefoil
base_elements.norse: rune stone fragment, knotwork interlace, mead horn
base_elements.industrial: gear, rivet, steam cloud, railway sleeper
